walkthrough.title: Choose Your Stuffy
walkthrough.text: Welcome to Stuffed Fables, brave protectors! First, each player chooses a stuffy character card and its matching miniature figure. There are six lovable stuffed animals to choose from: Theadora the bear, Lumpy the elephant, Flops the bunny, Stitch the old doll, Piggle the piglet, and Lionel the lion. Each stuffy has a unique active ability printed at the top of their card, plus earned abilities they can unlock by spending hearts. If this is your first story (The Big Girl Bed), note that Piggle and Lionel are not yet available -- you have not met them yet! In a 2-player game, each player may control 2 stuffies, treating each one as a separate player with its own turn.

walkthrough.title: Place Stuffing Tokens
walkthrough.text: Each stuffy starts the adventure with 5 stuffing tokens placed on their character card, light side up. Stuffing is your health -- it is what keeps your cuddly friend standing tall. If a stuffy ever loses all its stuffing, it collapses (tips over on its side). Think of stuffing as the courage and fluffiness that keeps each stuffy going through the dark and scary night.

walkthrough.title: Fill the Dice Bag
walkthrough.text: Gather all the attribute dice (purple, blue, green, red, and yellow) and drop them into the dice bag. Then count the number of stuffies in play, add 1, and put that many white stuffing dice into the bag. Add the same number of black threat dice. For example, with 3 stuffies you would add 4 white dice and 4 black dice. The bag is where you will draw your dice each turn -- a little grab bag of fate!

walkthrough.title: Prepare the Card Decks
walkthrough.text: Shuffle the item cards and form an item deck. Shuffle the Lost cards and form a Lost deck. Take the boss minion cards and set them face-up in their own pile, then shuffle the remaining minion cards into a separate minion deck. When setting up for your very first game, you will not have any boss minion cards yet -- those fearsome foes appear later in the story.

walkthrough.title: Build the Sleep Deck
walkthrough.text: The sleep deck is a clever little timer that determines how the story might end. Take the Waking sleep card and shuffle it together with 2 randomly chosen sleep cards, then set them face-down. Shuffle all remaining sleep cards and place them face-down on top. This means the Waking card is always somewhere in the bottom three cards. Each time a stuffy collapses or the Storybook instructs you, reveal a sleep card. If the Waking card appears, the ending of the story may change!

walkthrough.title: Set Out Tokens and Status Cards
walkthrough.text: Place the supply of status cards (worried, scared, angry, torn, etc.), buttons, extra stuffing tokens, heart tokens, objective tokens, and Lost tokens within easy reach of all players. You will be using these throughout the adventure. Buttons are your currency for shopping, hearts power your earned abilities, and status cards track conditions affecting your stuffies.

walkthrough.title: Position the Storybook and Sideboard
walkthrough.text: Place the Storybook in the center of the play area -- it serves as both your narrative guide and your game board, since the maps are printed right on its pages. Set the sideboard to the right side of the Storybook. The sideboard has the group task track at the top and the threat track running down its length. This is where black threat dice will accumulate during the game.

walkthrough.title: Choose the Bookkeeper and Gather Discovery Cards
walkthrough.text: Choose a player to be the first bookkeeper and give them the bookmark token. The bookkeeper reads the story aloud and takes the first turn; play then proceeds to the left. Open the Storybook to your chosen story's start page. If this is your first game, start with Story 1: The Big Girl Bed. Gather the discovery cards for that story (marked with a divider in the discovery deck) and place them face-down near the play area without looking at them. Now read the first section aloud and let the adventure begin!

Story 1 restricts to Theadora, Lumpy, Flops, and Stitch only
In a 2-player game, each player may control 2 stuffies

Add white stuffing dice = number of stuffies + 1
Add black threat dice = same number as white dice

Shuffle Lost cards into a Lost deck
Boss minion cards face-up in a pile; shuffle remaining minions into a minion deck

summary.title: Build the Sleep Deck

Shuffle remaining sleep cards on top
Waking card is always in the bottom 3 cards of the deck

summary.title: Set Out Tokens and Status Cards

Objective tokens and Lost tokens accessible to all players

Sideboard to the right with group task track and threat track

summary.title: Choose the Bookkeeper and Begin
bullets: Give the bookmark token to the first bookkeeper
Play proceeds to the left from the bookkeeper
Gather discovery cards for the chosen story (face-down, no peeking)
Open the Storybook and read the first section aloud

walkthrough.title: Turn Overview: Six Steps
walkthrough.text: Each player's turn follows six steps in order: (1) Draw Dice, (2) Find Stuffing, (3) Place Threat, (4) Perform Actions, (5) Discard Dice, and (6) Check Threat. Think of it as reaching into the bag, patching yourself up, watching the danger grow, then doing your best to help the team before the minions get their chance. Let's walk through each step.

walkthrough.title: Step 1 -- Draw Dice
walkthrough.text: Draw 5 dice from the dice bag and place them in front of you. This is your handful of possibilities for the turn. If fewer than 5 dice remain in the bag, draw what is left, return all dice from the discard pool back to the bag, then finish drawing up to 5. You never know what colors you will get -- that is part of the adventure!

walkthrough.title: Step 2 -- Find Stuffing
walkthrough.text: Roll any white stuffing dice you drew. You may roll them one at a time or combine multiples into a single roll for a higher result. If the roll result equals or exceeds your stuffy's current number of stuffing tokens, you gain 1 stuffing and discard those dice to the discard pool. You can keep rolling remaining white dice if you have more. There is no cap on stuffing -- the fluffier the better!

walkthrough.title: Step 3 -- Place Threat
walkthrough.text: Place all black threat dice you drew onto the threat track on the sideboard, filling from the top down. These ominous dice represent growing danger. They accumulate until there are enough to trigger a minion turn or a surge. You cannot avoid placing them -- the darkness is always creeping closer.

walkthrough.title: Step 4 -- Perform Actions (Movement)
walkthrough.text: Now spend your remaining colored attribute dice to perform actions. To move, roll any color die and move your stuffy that many spaces (orthogonally and/or diagonally) across dotted lines. To cross a solid colored line, you must use a die matching that line's color (or a purple die). Stuffies can move through spaces with other stuffies but cannot end on them. They cannot move through minions or onto bracketed spaces unless the Storybook says otherwise. Solid white lines can never be crossed.

walkthrough.title: Step 4 -- Perform Actions (Combat)
walkthrough.text: To perform a melee attack, your stuffy must have a melee weapon equipped and be adjacent to a minion with no colored lines between them. Roll red dice -- if the total meets or exceeds the minion's defense, it is defeated! Remove its figure and card, and earn 1 button. Ranged attacks work similarly but use green dice and a ranged weapon, and can target minions up to the weapon's range in spaces away, crossing colored lines (except solid white). Defeating a leader or boss also earns a courageous status card.

walkthrough.title: Step 4 -- Other Actions (Reserve, Encourage, Search)
walkthrough.text: Instead of spending a die, you may reserve it by storing it on your stuffy's card (if the slot is empty). Reserved dice can defend against damage later or be spent on a future turn. To encourage another stuffy, give 1 of your stuffing to an adjacent stuffy, or store one of your dice on their card. To search, spend yellow dice and try to meet the map's search difficulty number -- success lets you draw an item card to equip or trade to a teammate.

walkthrough.title: Step 4 -- Skill Tests and Group Tasks
walkthrough.text: Skill tests appear in the Storybook, on status cards, environment cards, and Lost cards. Each has a color and difficulty number. Spend dice of the matching color; if your roll meets or exceeds the difficulty, you pass. If not, the fail effect triggers. You automatically fail if you cannot spend the right dice. Group tasks are similar but multiple stuffies can contribute dice across their turns, placing results on the group task track until the total meets or exceeds the difficulty. Group tasks cannot fail -- they just take time.

walkthrough.title: Purple Dice -- The Wild Card
walkthrough.text: Purple dice are wonderfully versatile. They can substitute for any other attribute die color -- use them to move, attack, search, or pass skill tests of any color. However, purple dice do not benefit from color-specific bonuses. For example, if a weapon says 'add +1 to red die results,' a purple die used for that attack would not get the bonus. Purple dice keep their own identity even when filling in for another color.

walkthrough.title: Steps 5 and 6 -- Discard and Check Threat
walkthrough.text: After performing all your actions, discard any remaining dice to the discard pool. Then check the threat track. If minions are in play and the threat dice on the track equal or exceed the number of minions, a minion turn happens (see next step). If no minions are in play and the threat dice equal or exceed the number of stuffies, a surge occurs -- resolve the surge effect listed on the current Storybook page.

walkthrough.title: Minion Turn
walkthrough.text: During a minion turn, activate each minion one at a time starting from the top of the minion column. For each: take a die off the threat track, roll it, and match the result to an action on the minion's card (this sets its speed, range, and strength). Move the minion toward the nearest stuffy up to its speed, then it attacks. Damage equals the minion's strength. The target stuffy can defend by rolling a stored die to block damage up to the roll result. Unblocked damage removes stuffing. After all minions activate, discard remaining threat dice and return all discarded dice to the bag.

walkthrough.title: Collapsing, Sleep Cards, and the Waking Card
walkthrough.text: When a stuffy loses all its stuffing, it collapses -- tip its figure on its side and discard all its status cards, buttons, and hearts. A collapsed stuffy cannot act, store dice, or use items. It still takes turns and can recover by finding stuffing with white dice or being encouraged by a friend. Each collapse also triggers a sleep card reveal. Asleep cards have no effect. Restless cards may trigger effects from the Storybook sidebar. If the Waking card is revealed, no more sleep cards are drawn, and the story's Waking ending will play if the stuffies win.

walkthrough.title: Exploring the Map -- Points of Interest, The Lost, and Vendors
walkthrough.text: As your stuffy moves across the Storybook map, watch for special symbols. Points of interest stop your movement and direct you to read a Storybook entry -- these are story moments with choices and consequences. Lost tokens let you draw a Lost card, encountering one of the lost toys of the Fall who may need your help. Vendor symbols let you shop: reveal 4 item cards and spend 1 button or discard 1 item per card you want. Moving to a new page clears remaining vendor items.

walkthrough.title: Winning and Losing
walkthrough.text: The stuffies win by completing the story objectives as directed by the Storybook. Each story has its own goals -- rescuing someone, reaching a destination, defeating a great evil. If all stuffies are collapsed at the same time, the players lose and the nightmare wins. The sleep deck adds another layer: whether the Waking card was revealed determines which ending you experience. Every choice matters, and the story remembers your bravery (or your stumbles).

walkthrough.title: Welcome to Your First Night
walkthrough.text: For your practice game, we recommend 3 players, each controlling one stuffy. Choose Theadora, Lumpy, and Flops (or swap one for Stitch). Open the Storybook to Story 1: The Big Girl Bed. This is designed as the introductory adventure and gradually teaches core mechanics. Read the opening story aloud -- let the bookkeeper set the mood!

walkthrough.title: Set Up the Dice Bag
walkthrough.text: With 3 stuffies, place all attribute dice in the bag plus 4 white stuffing dice and 4 black threat dice (3 stuffies + 1 each). Double-check the count -- having the right ratio of white and black dice is important for game balance. Too many threat dice speeds up danger; too few white dice makes recovery harder.

walkthrough.title: The Bookkeeper Draws First
walkthrough.text: The bookkeeper draws 5 dice from the bag. Let's say you pull: 1 red, 1 yellow, 1 white, 1 black, and 1 purple. Separate them by type in front of you. White dice are for finding stuffing, black dice go on the threat track, and the colored ones are your action dice for the turn.

walkthrough.title: Practice Finding Stuffing
walkthrough.text: You start with 5 stuffing. To gain more with your white die, you need to roll a 5 or higher (matching or exceeding your current stuffing count). Roll the white die. If you get a 5 or 6, hooray -- place another stuffing token on your card and discard the white die. If you roll lower, the die is still discarded but you gain nothing. Early in the game this is tough; later when you have taken damage and your stuffing is low, it becomes easier to recover.

walkthrough.title: Practice Placing Threat
walkthrough.text: Take your black die and place it on the first open slot of the threat track on the sideboard. No rolling -- just place it. Watch how threat builds up over multiple turns. With 3 stuffies and no minions on the board, a surge will trigger once 3 or more black dice sit on the threat track. Keep an eye on this!

walkthrough.title: Practice Moving Your Stuffy
walkthrough.text: Now use one of your colored dice to move. Let's use the red die. Roll it -- say you get a 4. Move your stuffy up to 4 spaces on the map grid. Move diagonally or in straight lines, counting each space. Remember: dotted lines are free to cross with any color, but solid colored lines require a die of that specific color (or purple). Try navigating toward a point of interest or a Lost token.

walkthrough.title: Practice Using Purple Dice as Wildcards
walkthrough.text: You have a purple die left. This versatile die can substitute for any color. Need to cross a green line? Use it as green. Want to search? Use it as yellow. Want to attack in melee? Use it as red. Roll it and apply the result as though it were whatever color you need most. Just remember it will not get color-specific weapon bonuses.

walkthrough.title: Practice Searching
walkthrough.text: If the map has a search difficulty (shown in the upper right corner), you can spend yellow dice to search. Roll the yellow die -- if the result meets or exceeds the target number, draw an item card! You can equip it (check the equipment slot icon) or hand it to a teammate. If you do not have yellow dice, a purple die works too. Searching is how your team gears up with weapons, armor, and gadgets.

walkthrough.title: Practice Reserving a Die for Defense
walkthrough.text: If you have a die you do not want to spend right now, you can reserve it instead of rolling it. Place it unrolled on your stuffy's die storage slot. Later, when a minion attacks you, you can roll this stored die to block damage equal to the result. If you block ALL the damage, the die stays on your card! This is your stuffed shield. You can only store one die at a time.

walkthrough.title: Practice the Encourage Action
walkthrough.text: Encouraging is how you support your fluffy friends. You can give 1 of your stuffing tokens to any stuffy (including a collapsed one to revive them!), or you can store one of your dice on another stuffy's card so they have a defense die ready. This is especially important when a teammate is low on stuffing or has no reserved die against incoming attacks.

walkthrough.title: Encountering a Point of Interest
walkthrough.text: When your stuffy enters or passes over a point-of-interest symbol on the map, stop immediately and read the matching entry in the Storybook. These are branching story moments -- you might find a scared toy, discover a hidden passage, or face a tricky choice. Some choices lead to skill tests. These are the heart of the narrative experience, so take your time with them.

walkthrough.title: Meeting the Lost
walkthrough.text: When a stuffy lands on a space with a Lost token, discard the token. The player to the left draws a Lost card and reads it aloud. You will meet lost toys who are citizens of the Fall -- a world beneath the bed. They often present a choice: help them (sometimes requiring a skill test or spending resources) or walk away. Helping usually rewards you, but sometimes a heartfelt thanks is all you get. That is the nature of kindness.

walkthrough.title: Practice a Skill Test
walkthrough.text: Suppose the Storybook says: 'Perform a yellow skill test, difficulty 4.' You must spend yellow (or purple) dice and roll them. If the total is 4 or higher, you pass and get the good outcome. If it is lower, or you have no appropriate dice, you fail. Some skill tests have consequences for failing, so consider whether it is worth the attempt. You automatically fail if you simply do not have the right color dice available.

walkthrough.title: Triggering a Surge
walkthrough.text: With no minions on the board, once 3+ black dice accumulate on the threat track (equaling or exceeding the number of stuffies), a surge triggers. Read the surge instructions on the current Storybook page. The first surge on a page usually has a unique effect (like generating an encounter or advancing the story). Additional surges on the same page typically reveal a sleep card. After resolving: discard all threat dice and return discarded dice to the bag.

walkthrough.title: Your First Encounter
walkthrough.text: When the Storybook tells you to generate an encounter, shuffle the minion deck and deal cards equal to the number of stuffies (3 cards for 3 stuffies). Place them face-up in a column near the sideboard -- this is the minion column that determines activation order. Put the matching minion figures on or adjacent to the spaces marked with the minion start symbol on the map. The bookkeeper chooses exact placement.

walkthrough.title: Practice Melee Combat
walkthrough.text: To attack a minion in melee, your stuffy needs a melee weapon equipped and must be adjacent to the target with no solid colored lines in between. Roll red dice (you can roll multiple for a higher total). Check the minion card's defense value -- if your total meets or exceeds it, the minion is defeated! Remove its figure and card. You earn 1 button. Colored lines that only partially block the path (do not fully separate the spaces) do not prevent melee attacks.

walkthrough.title: Practice the Minion Turn
walkthrough.text: When threat dice trigger a minion turn, activate minions one at a time from the top of the column. Take a black die off the threat track, roll it, and match the number to an action on the minion's card. That action tells you its speed, range, and strength. Move it toward the nearest stuffy (up to its speed), then it attacks the closest stuffy in range, dealing damage equal to its strength. The target can roll a stored die to block. After all minions activate, discard remaining threat dice and refresh the dice bag.

walkthrough.title: Practice Defending Against Damage
walkthrough.text: When your stuffy takes damage (from a minion or story event), you can roll the die stored on your character card to defend. The roll result blocks that much damage. If you block ALL the damage, your die returns to storage -- ready for next time! If any damage gets through, the die is discarded and you lose stuffing equal to the unblocked amount. This is why reserving high-value dice is so valuable.

walkthrough.title: Common Mistake #1 -- Forgetting to Place Threat Dice
walkthrough.text: A very common mistake is forgetting to place black dice on the threat track before performing actions. It is easy to get excited about your colored dice and skip straight to actions. Always follow the turn order: Draw, Find Stuffing, Place Threat, THEN Perform Actions. Skipping threat placement makes the game artificially easier and throws off the tension that makes each decision meaningful.

walkthrough.title: Common Mistake #2 -- Using Purple Dice with Color Bonuses
walkthrough.text: Purple dice are wonderful wildcards, but remember: they do not get color-specific bonuses. If your Meat Mallet says 'add +1 to red die results,' a purple die used for that melee attack does NOT get the +1. The purple die substitutes for the color to let you perform the action, but it stays purple for bonus purposes. This trips up many new players!

walkthrough.title: Common Mistake #3 -- Moving Through Minions
walkthrough.text: Stuffies can move through spaces occupied by other stuffies (they just cannot end there), but they absolutely cannot move through a space containing a minion. You must go around! This is important when minions block corridors or stand between you and a teammate who needs help. Plan your routes carefully and consider ranged attacks or having a friend clear the way.

walkthrough.title: Exploring a Vendor
walkthrough.text: When a stuffy first enters a vendor space (marked with the vendor symbol), stop and reveal 4 cards from the item deck. Any stuffy on or adjacent to the vendor space can shop. Spend 1 button or discard 1 item card for each item you want to buy. Leftover vendor items stay available until play moves to a new Storybook page, then get shuffled back. Coordinate with your team about who needs what gear!

walkthrough.title: Using Hearts and Earned Abilities
walkthrough.text: Hearts are earned through story accomplishments. You spend a heart to activate one of the earned abilities listed below the line on your stuffy's card. Each stuffy's earned abilities are powerful and unique -- Stitch can distract minions, Lumpy can absorb damage for friends, Theadora can remove status cards. Your active ability (above the line) can be used for free without spending a heart. Time your abilities for maximum impact!

walkthrough.title: Leaving a Page and Moving Forward
walkthrough.text: When the Storybook directs you to turn to a new page, finish the current player's turn first. Then remove all minions and tokens from the current page, move all stuffy figures back to their character cards, and set up the new page as directed. Discovery cards remain face-down for later retrieval. The story continues, and your gear, stuffing, and statuses carry over -- you are the same brave stuffies, just in a new scene.

walkthrough.title: You Are Ready to Protect the Little Girl!
walkthrough.text: You now know everything you need to play through Story 1 and beyond. Remember: this is a cooperative game, so talk to each other. Discuss who should move where, who should reserve a die for defense, and who can encourage a struggling friend. The story will guide you with choices that matter, and your stuffies' bravery will determine whether the little girl sleeps soundly. Go forth, brave stuffies -- the Nightmare King awaits!

summary.title: Welcome to Your First Night
bullets: Recommended: 3 players with Theadora, Lumpy, Flops (or Stitch)
Start with Story 1: The Big Girl Bed

bullets: All attribute dice + 4 white + 4 black for 3 stuffies

bullets: Draw 5 dice; separate by color: white for stuffing, black for threat, colored for actions

bullets: Roll white die >= current stuffing count to gain 1
Easier to recover when stuffing is low

Matching color needed for solid colored lines

No color-specific bonuses apply

summary.title: Points of Interest

Help or walk away; rewards vary

Auto-fail if no appropriate dice available

summary.title: Triggering a Surge

First surge has unique page effect; additional surges reveal sleep card

summary.title: First Encounter

summary.title: Melee Combat

Roll >= defense to defeat; earn 1 button

summary.title: Defending

summary.title: Mistake #1 -- Forgetting Threat
bullets: Always place black dice on threat track BEFORE taking actions

summary.title: Mistake #2 -- Purple + Bonuses
bullets: Purple dice do NOT receive color-specific bonuses from weapons or effects

summary.title: Mistake #3 -- Moving Through Minions
bullets: Stuffies CANNOT move through minion-occupied spaces; go around them

summary.title: Vendor Shopping
bullets: Reveal 4 items; pay 1 button or 1 discarded item per purchase

summary.title: Hearts and Earned Abilities
bullets: Spend hearts for earned abilities; active ability is free
Each stuffy has unique powerful abilities

summary.title: Leaving a Page
bullets: Finish current turn, remove minions/tokens, return stuffies to cards, set up new page

summary.title: You Are Ready!
bullets: Communicate as a team, plan movement and defense together
The Storybook guides you -- let the adventure unfold!

walkthrough.title: Always Reserve a Die When Possible
walkthrough.text: One of the best habits in Stuffed Fables is ending your action phase with a die stored on your stuffy's card. A reserved die is your only defense against minion attacks and story damage. Even a small die is better than nothing -- blocking even 1 or 2 damage can mean the difference between staying fluffy and collapsing into a heap. If you have a high-value colored die left over, reserve it rather than spending it on a mediocre move.

walkthrough.title: Communicate and Coordinate Every Turn
walkthrough.text: This is a cooperative game, so talk constantly. Before each player takes their turn, discuss as a group. Who is closest to the objective? Who is low on stuffing? Is a minion turn imminent? Should someone encourage a weakened teammate instead of attacking? The stuffies who survive the night are the ones who work together. Share information about what dice you drew so the team can plan around available resources.

walkthrough.title: Manage the Threat Track Proactively
walkthrough.text: Keep a mental count of how many black dice are on the threat track and compare it to the number of minions (or stuffies if no minions are present). If a minion turn or surge is about to trigger, plan accordingly. Sometimes it is better to defeat a minion quickly to change the threat threshold. Other times, you might want to let a surge happen to advance the story. Understanding this tempo is key to controlling the game's pacing.

walkthrough.title: Prioritize Equipping Weapons Early
walkthrough.text: A stuffy without a weapon cannot make melee or ranged attacks. Searching early (spending yellow dice to meet the map's search difficulty) is a high priority. Once your team has at least one melee weapon and one ranged weapon distributed among the group, you can handle encounters much more comfortably. Do not let the whole team walk into a fight unarmed -- search first, then push forward.

walkthrough.title: Use the Encourage Action to Save Friends
walkthrough.text: Encouraging is one of the most important actions in the game. If a teammate's stuffy is collapsed, the fastest way to revive them is for another stuffy to give them 1 stuffing through encouragement. You can also store a die on their card for defense. Do not underestimate this action -- a well-timed encourage can pull the team back from the brink and keep the adventure going.

walkthrough.title: Read Lost Cards Thoughtfully
walkthrough.text: Lost cards present you with choices, and the game specifically says DO NOT READ THE RESULT UNTIL THE CHOICE HAS BEEN MADE. Discuss the choice as a group, but commit before peeking at the outcome. Helping the Lost often costs resources but rewards hearts, items, or other benefits. Sometimes it rewards nothing tangible -- just the warm feeling of being kind. In a game about stuffed animals protecting a child, that counts for a lot.

walkthrough.title: Watch the Sleep Deck Carefully
walkthrough.text: The sleep deck is a soft timer on the game. Each stuffy collapse reveals a card, and the Waking card hides in the bottom three. If you are collapsing frequently, you will burn through sleep cards fast and potentially trigger the Waking card early, which changes your ending. Try to minimize collapses not just for survival, but to control which ending your story gets. The Asleep ending and the Waking ending can be quite different!

walkthrough.title: Embrace the Story and the Choices
walkthrough.text: Stuffed Fables is as much a narrative experience as it is a tactical game. Take time to read the story aloud with feeling. Discuss choices in character. Let younger players make decisions about whether to help the Lost or take risks. The game's magic comes from the emotional investment in these brave little stuffed animals. Do not rush through the text to get to the next dice roll -- the journey IS the game.

summary.title: Always Reserve a Die When Possible
bullets: A stored die is your only defense against attacks
High-value dice make the best defensive reserves

summary.title: Communicate Every Turn
bullets: Discuss dice draws, movement plans, and who needs help
Cooperative planning is the team's greatest strength

summary.title: Manage the Threat Track
bullets: Count threat dice vs. minions/stuffies to predict minion turns and surges
Defeat minions or adjust timing to control pacing

summary.title: Equip Weapons Early
bullets: Search (yellow dice) early to find melee and ranged weapons
An unarmed stuffy cannot fight back in combat

summary.title: Use Encourage Generously
bullets: Give stuffing to collapsed teammates to revive them
Store defensive dice on vulnerable friends

summary.title: Read Lost Cards Thoughtfully
bullets: Make your choice BEFORE reading the outcome
Helping the Lost often rewards hearts and items

summary.title: Watch the Sleep Deck
bullets: Minimize collapses to slow sleep card reveals
Waking card in bottom 3 determines alternate ending

summary.title: Embrace the Story
bullets: Read aloud with feeling; discuss choices in character
The narrative experience is the heart of the game

walkthrough.title: Dice Economy -- Counting the Bag
walkthrough.text: Advanced players track roughly what remains in the dice bag. If many black dice have already been drawn, subsequent draws will be lighter on threat. If the bag was just refreshed from the discard pool, expect a more balanced draw. You can estimate the ratio of attribute dice to threat and stuffing dice based on what has been drawn and discarded. This lets you plan whether a teammate should hold off on spending dice or whether a surge is imminent.

walkthrough.title: Stuffy Synergies -- Building Your Team
walkthrough.text: Each stuffy's abilities complement different playstyles. Stitch's Distract ability can skip a minion activation entirely by spending a stored yellow die -- pair him with Lumpy, whose Protect ability lets him absorb damage for others. Theadora's Versatile ability lets her swap an attribute die for one in the discard pool each turn, giving her unmatched flexibility. Piggle's Blessing of the Child can give another stuffy a clutch purple die mid-action. Build your team composition around these synergies.

walkthrough.title: Threat Manipulation and Surge Timing
walkthrough.text: Sometimes you want a surge to happen. The first surge on a page often advances the story or triggers a specific event you need. If no minions are in play, you can predict when a surge will occur by counting threat dice versus the number of stuffies. Conversely, if minions are in play and you are not ready for a minion turn, focus on defeating one minion to raise the threshold. Skilled players time threat buildup to control when enemies act.

walkthrough.title: Efficient Movement and Positioning
walkthrough.text: Movement costs dice, which could be spent on other actions. Plan routes that require the fewest dice possible. If you need to cross a green solid line, save a green die (or purple) specifically for that move rather than spending it elsewhere. Position stuffies so that at least one is always adjacent to a minion for melee while another stays at range. Avoid clustering where a single area-of-effect event could damage everyone. Spread out but stay within encourage distance.

walkthrough.title: Maximize the Value of Purple Dice
walkthrough.text: Purple dice are the most flexible resource in the game. Do not waste them on low-value actions like moving 1 space across a dotted line when any die could do that. Save purple dice for crossing solid colored lines you have no matching dice for, passing skill tests of unusual colors, or contributing to a group task where the color requirement would otherwise lock you out. Their power is in filling gaps, not duplicating what other dice already handle.

walkthrough.title: Boss Fight Preparation
walkthrough.text: Boss minions have dark stuffing tokens equal to the number of stuffies in play, meaning each 'defeat' only removes one token. Plan for a prolonged fight. Stock up on items and hearts before the boss encounter. Have at least two stuffies with weapons. Use Stitch's Distract to skip the boss's activation on dangerous rolls. Reserve high-value dice for defense -- bosses hit hard. Defeating a boss also grants a courageous status card, which is a nice reward for the effort.

walkthrough.title: Managing Status Cards
walkthrough.text: Status effects like Worried, Scared, Angry, and Torn can cripple a stuffy. Worried prevents storing or using purple dice. Scared limits movement. Each status card tells you how to remove it (usually a skill test of a specific color and difficulty). Prioritize removing statuses from your damage-dealer or from whichever stuffy is most impacted. Theadora's Persevere earned ability can remove statuses without a skill test. Piggle's Positivity clears all Worried statuses from the entire team at once.

walkthrough.title: Vendor Strategy and Equipment Optimization
walkthrough.text: When you encounter a vendor, reveal 4 item cards. Before anyone buys, discuss as a team who needs what. Equipment slots (Head, Body, Paws, Accessory) limit what each stuffy can carry -- equipping a new item in an occupied slot forces you to discard the old one. Consider whether a party item (usable by everyone) is worth more than a personal upgrade. Save buttons for vendors with strong offerings rather than spending them as soon as you earn them.

walkthrough.title: The Find Stuffing Gambit
walkthrough.text: The Find Stuffing step has a subtle strategic layer. When your stuffing is high (4-5), you need a high roll on white dice to gain more, so rolling multiple white dice together for a combined result can be worthwhile. When your stuffing is low (1-2), even a single white die can succeed on most rolls. Experienced players sometimes deliberately stay at moderate stuffing levels rather than over-investing in recovery, since those white dice are essentially 'dead' for action purposes anyway. Balance recovery with accepting risk.

walkthrough.title: Campaign Continuity and Discovery Cards
walkthrough.text: Between stories, any discovery cards that were not found during a story get added to their respective decks, making them available in future stories. This means items, minions, and encounters build up over the campaign. Pay attention to what the discovery deck might contain and plan for surprises. If you missed a helpful item in Story 1, it may appear in the item deck for Story 2. The campaign grows richer as you play, so treat each story as a chapter in a larger saga.

bullets: Combine white dice for high stuffing; roll individually when low
Accept moderate stuffing to save turn efficiency

The campaign's item and minion pools grow over time

entries.definition: The ability printed at the top of a stuffy's character card that can be used without spending a heart.
The 8 spaces immediately surrounding a given space (orthogonal and diagonal). Used for melee attacks, encourage, and various effects.
The colored dice (red, green, yellow, blue, purple) used to perform actions on your turn.
See Threat Dice. Black dice placed on the threat track that trigger minion turns or surges when they accumulate.
Given to the current bookkeeper to indicate turn order. The bookkeeper takes the first turn.
The player who reads the Storybook aloud and takes the first turn each round. Passes to the left when a new story or session begins.
A powerful enemy with dark stuffing tokens equal to the number of stuffies in play. Must be 'defeated' once per token before being removed.
A space on the map used for counting range but that cannot be moved onto unless the Storybook specifically directs it.
Currency earned when defeating a minion. Can be spent at vendors or as directed by the game.
The card for each stuffy showing its name, active ability, earned abilities, and storage slots for dice, stuffing, and hearts.
A stuffy that has lost all its stuffing. It tips on its side, discards all statuses/buttons/hearts, and cannot act until it regains stuffing.
Lines on the map grid that restrict movement. Solid colored lines require a matching color (or purple) die to cross. Solid white lines cannot be crossed at all.
A status card earned by defeating a leader or boss minion. A positive status with beneficial effects.
The Nightmare King. The primary antagonist who sends minions to threaten the little girl in her sleep.
Stuffing tokens placed dark-side-up on boss minion cards. One is removed each time the boss would be defeated; the boss is truly defeated only when all are gone.
Roll a die stored on your character card to block damage. If ALL damage is blocked, the die returns to your card; otherwise it is discarded.
The target number for a skill test or group task. Your roll result must meet or exceed this number to pass.
The area where spent dice are placed. When the bag runs low, all dice from the discard pool return to the bag.
Pre-arranged cards divided by story. Gathered face-down at the start of each story and revealed as the Storybook directs. Do not look through or rearrange them.
Lines on the map that any color die can cross freely when moving.
Abilities listed below the line on a character card. Each costs 1 heart to activate.
When minions are brought into play, either as a random encounter (shuffled minion deck) or a named encounter (specific minions listed by the Storybook).
An action where you give 1 stuffing to another stuffy or store one of your dice on another stuffy's card.
Cards that modify the current map's rules, such as Darkness/Low Visibility which reduces weapon range to 1.
Each stuffy has 4 slots: Head, Body, Paws, and Accessory. Only 1 item per slot; equipping a new item in an occupied slot discards the old one.
The mysterious world beneath the bed where the Lost reside. The setting for the stuffies' adventures.
A miniature representing a stuffy or minion on the Storybook map.
Step 2 of a player's turn. Roll white dice; if the result meets or exceeds your current stuffing count, gain 1 stuffing.
A task multiple stuffies can contribute to across turns by rolling appropriate colored dice. Results accumulate on the group task track. Cannot fail, only takes time.
The track on the sideboard where dice results are accumulated toward completing a group task's difficulty.
A token earned through story accomplishments. Spent to use an earned ability on your character card.
Cards found by searching or at vendors. Equipped to a stuffy's equipment slot. Includes weapons, armor, and accessories.
A stronger type of minion. If available during encounter generation, a leader must be included. Defeating one grants a courageous status.
Cards representing encounters with lost toys in the Fall. Drawn when a stuffy lands on a Lost token. Offer choices with various outcomes.
Placed on map spaces. When a stuffy enters or passes over, discard the token and the player to the left draws a Lost card.
An attack using red dice against an adjacent minion. Requires a melee weapon. Roll result must meet or exceed the minion's defense.
Shows a minion's name, type, actions (keyed to die results), defense value, and miniature base shape.
The ordered column of face-up minion cards created during an encounter. Determines activation order from top to bottom.
Triggered when threat dice on the track equal or exceed the number of minions in play. Each minion activates once in column order.
An encounter that specifies exactly which minions and how many enter play, as directed by the Storybook.
The basic type of enemy minion. Defeated in a single successful attack.
An item that is not equipped to any single stuffy but placed in reach of all players. Treated as equipped to all stuffies simultaneously.
Special symbols on the map. When a stuffy enters or passes over one, stop movement and read the matching Storybook entry.
Wild attribute dice that substitute for any other color. Do not benefit from color-specific bonuses.
An encounter generated by shuffling the minion deck and dealing cards equal to the number of stuffies in play.
An attack using green dice with a ranged weapon. Can target minions up to the weapon's range in spaces, crossing colored lines (except solid white).
The maximum number of spaces away a ranged weapon can target. Printed on ranged weapon item cards.
A player aid card summarizing key rules and turn steps for quick reference during play.
Store an unrolled die on your stuffy's die storage slot instead of spending it. Can be used for defense or spent on a future turn. Only 1 die can be reserved at a time.
Spend yellow dice. If the roll meets or exceeds the map's search difficulty, draw an item card.
The board placed to the right of the Storybook containing the group task track and the threat track.
A test requiring specific colored dice rolled against a difficulty number. Pass if the roll meets or exceeds the target; otherwise fail.
Revealed when a stuffy collapses or the Storybook directs. Asleep cards have no effect. Restless cards may trigger sidebar effects.
A deck with the Waking card hidden in the bottom 3. Acts as a soft timer for the game's narrative endings.
Lines on the map that cannot be crossed by any die, stuffy, or minion under any circumstances.
A condition placed on a stuffy (Worried, Scared, Angry, Torn, Courageous, Soggy, etc.). Each has an effect and a removal condition.
The central component serving as both narrative guide and game board. Contains maps, story text, and page-specific rules.
A stuffed animal character controlled by a player. The brave protectors of the little girl.
Health points for a stuffy. Starts at 5, can go higher with no cap. Losing all stuffing causes collapse.
White dice drawn from the bag. Rolled during the Find Stuffing step to potentially gain stuffing.
Triggered when there are no minions in play and threat dice on the track meet or exceed the number of stuffies. Resolves the page's surge effect, then discards threat and refreshes the bag.
Black dice placed on the threat track during step 3 of each turn. Accumulate to trigger minion turns or surges.
The track on the sideboard where black threat dice are placed. When full enough, triggers minion turns or surges.
A map symbol where stuffies can shop. Reveal 4 item cards; buy with 1 button or 1 discarded item each.
A special sleep card hidden in the bottom 3 of the sleep deck. If revealed, no more sleep cards are drawn and the story's Waking ending triggers if the stuffies win.
See Stuffing Dice. Used during the Find Stuffing step to recover health.
